Package yams.mechanisms.positional

Class SmartPositionalMechanism

java.lang.Object
yams.mechanisms.SmartMechanism
yams.mechanisms.positional.SmartPositionalMechanism
Direct Known Subclasses:
Arm, DifferentialMechanism, DoubleJointedArm, Elevator, Pivot
public abstract class SmartPositionalMechanism extends SmartMechanism
Abstract intermediate base class for all closed-loop positional mechanisms in YAMS.

SmartPositionalMechanism sits between SmartMechanism and the concrete positional mechanism implementations such as Arm, Elevator, Pivot, and DifferentialMechanism. It provides the shared infrastructure required for closed-loop position control:

  • Setpoint management via SmartMechanism.setMechanismPositionSetpoint(edu.wpi.first.units.measure.Angle) and SmartMechanism.setMeasurementPositionSetpoint(edu.wpi.first.units.measure.Distance)
  • Position-based Trigger factories (isNear(), gte(), lte(), between(), max(), min()) — defined by each concrete subclass
  • Command factories such as setAngle() and setHeight() defined by each concrete subclass, which internally call SmartMechanism.setMechanismPositionSetpoint(edu.wpi.first.units.measure.Angle) or SmartMechanism.setMeasurementPositionSetpoint(edu.wpi.first.units.measure.Distance)
  • A 2D visualization model via MechanismRoot2d and MechanismLigament2d fields that subclasses populate and update

Concrete mechanisms extend this class and supply the physics-specific details: how to read the current position, how to drive the simulation model, and how to update visualization and telemetry. The setpoint infrastructure and WPILib subsystem integration live here so that all positional mechanisms share a consistent, command-based API without code duplication.

Conceptual subclass pattern: A concrete positional mechanism extends SmartPositionalMechanism, provides a typed command factory, and calls into the base-class setpoint methods: 


 // A concrete mechanism delegates setpoint management to this base class:
 public Command setAngle(Angle target) {
     return Commands.startRun(
         () -> setMechanismPositionSetpoint(target),
         () -> {},
         m_subsystem
     ).withName(getName() + " SetAngle");
 }

For Subclass Authors

Every concrete subclass of SmartPositionalMechanism must implement the following abstract methods inherited from SmartMechanism:

  • SmartMechanism.getRelativeMechanismPosition() — returns the current 3-D position of the mechanism end-point in Mechanism2d coordinates
  • SmartMechanism.visualizationUpdate() — updates m_mechanismLigament (and any setpoint ligament) to reflect the current state
  • SmartMechanism.getName() — returns a human-readable mechanism name used for telemetry and command names
  • SmartMechanism.simIterate() — advances the physics simulation model and writes back simulated encoder values each robot loop
  • SmartMechanism.updateTelemetry() — publishes mechanism state to SmartDashboard or an equivalent telemetry sink
  • max() — returns a Trigger that activates when the mechanism reaches its configured maximum limit
  • min() — returns a Trigger that activates when the mechanism reaches its configured minimum limit

    • max

      public abstract Trigger max()
      SmartPositionalMechanism is at max, defined by the soft limit or hard limit on the SmartPositionalMechanism.

      Maximum angle for the SmartPositionalMechanism.

      public abstract Trigger min()
      Minimum angle of the SmartPositionalMechanism given by the soft limit or hard limit of the SmartPositionalMechanism.
      Returns:
      Trigger on minimum of the SmartPositionalMechanism.

    • getMechanismLigament

      public MechanismLigament2d getMechanismLigament()
      Get the ligament of the 2D mechanism model. Used to change the position of the mechanism model in the SmartDashboard.
      Returns:
      Ligament of the 2D mechanism model.
